message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior. Audit the test suite (tests/) for NON-DISCRIMINATING tests (pass whether or not the code is correct — verify by reverting the implementation and checking the test still passes), over-mocked tests that never exercise real logic, assertions weaker than the docstring/guarantee they claim to cover, and untested error/edge branches. Prioritize security/methodology/concurrency guards. For each, name the exact test and what it fails to catch.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me focus on the security/methodology/concurrency guards first. Let me read the key source files and their corresponding tests.

content.content:
1	"""The two firewalls, enforced as runtime primitives (ADR-0005, METHODOLOGY.md §2).
2	
3	Both separations are enforced in code and exercised by tests — never asserted only in prose.
4	
5	Firewall A — claim-blindness:
6	    The baseline must be built blind to external claims. The *structural* guarantee is that
7	    the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` (its ``build`` signature
8	    takes only a ``Corpus``) and that ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside
9	    adjudication mode. On top of that, :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` gives the claims source
10	    *custody*: hand the source to the guard, and the only way to obtain it is
11	    ``release()``/``access()``, which raise while the guard is sealed. Baseline construction
12	    runs inside ``sealed()``; adjudication runs after it.
13	
14	    Caveat (audit pass 1, issue #1): the guard cannot police a baseline builder that ignores
15	    it and reads external judgments smuggled into ``Corpus`` metadata/relations — that path
16	    is closed by the adapter contract (see schema.py), not by this guard. The guard is
17	    custodial defense-in-depth over the type-level separation, not the sole barrier.
18	
19	Firewall B — discover/confirm separation:
20	    The data used to discover a hypothesis must not be the data used to confirm it.
21	    :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit` holds disjoint, non-empty discovery and confirmation id
22	    sets and refuses to confirm on any id the discovery phase touched (or on an empty set).
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	from contextlib import contextmanager
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Generic, Iterable, Iterator, TypeVar
30	
31	C = TypeVar("C")
32	
33	
34	class FirewallViolation(RuntimeError):
35	    """Raised when a methodological firewall is breached at runtime."""
36	
37	
38	class ClaimBlindGuard(Generic[C]):
39	    """Firewall A. While sealed, the held claims source is unreachable.
40	
41	    Custodial usage (preferred) — the guard owns the source, so the baseline pipeline holds
42	    the guard rather than the source and cannot read claims while sealed::
43	
44	        guard = ClaimBlindGuard(claims_source)
45	        with guard.sealed():
46	            corpus = parser.parse(source)            # baseline build — claims unreachable
47	            baseline = build_baseline(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
48	        claims = guard.release()                      # adjudication — guard released
49	
50	    ``access(obj)`` remains for defensive checks at call sites that hold a reference directly:
51	    it returns ``obj`` when unsealed and raises while sealed.
52	    """
53	
54	    def __init__(self, claims_source: C | None = None) -> None:
55	        self._sealed = False
56	        self._claims_source = claims_source
57	
58	    @property
59	    def is_sealed(self) -> bool:
60	        return self._sealed
61	
62	    @contextmanager
63	    def sealed(self) -> Iterator["ClaimBlindGuard[C]"]:
64	        prior = self._sealed
65	        self._sealed = True
66	        try:
67	            yield self
68	        finally:
69	            self._sealed = prior
70	
71	    def release(self) -> C:
72	        """Return the custodial claims source, or raise while sealed / if none was given."""
73	        if self._sealed:
74	            raise FirewallViolation(
75	                "Firewall A: external claims released during blind baseline construction"
76	            )
77	        if self._claims_source is None:
78	            raise FirewallViolation("Firewall A: no claims source is under this guard's custody")
79	        return self._claims_source
80	
81	    def access(self, claims_source: C) -> C:
82	        """Return ``claims_source`` only if the guard is not sealed; else raise."""
83	        if self._sealed:
84	            raise FirewallViolation(
85	                "Firewall A: external claims accessed during blind baseline construction"
86	            )
87	        return claims_source
88	
89	
90	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
91	class DiscoverConfirmSplit:
92	    """Firewall B. Disjoint discovery / confirmation id partitions for unit-level claims."""
93	
94	    discovery_ids: frozenset[str]
95	    confirm_ids: frozenset[str]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        overlap = self.discovery_ids & self.confirm_ids
99	        if overlap:
100	            raise FirewallViolation(
101	                f"Firewall B: discovery and confirmation partitions overlap on "
102	                f"{len(overlap)} id(s), e.g. {sorted(overlap)[:3]}"
103	            )
104	        # An empty confirmation partition means there is nothing held out to confirm on; a
105	        # unit-level held-out split with no held-out object is invalid by construction. The
106	        # genuine no-holdout case must use the whole-corpus null path (audit pass 1, issue #5).
107	        if not self.confirm_ids:
108	            raise FirewallViolation(
109	                "Firewall B: confirmation partition is empty — a unit-level held-out split "
110	                "must hold out at least one object (use whole-corpus confirmation otherwise)"
111	            )
112	
113	    @classmethod
114	    def from_partition(
115	        cls, discovery_ids: Iterable[str], confirm_ids: Iterable[str]
116	    ) -> "DiscoverConfirmSplit":
117	        return cls(frozenset(discovery_ids), frozenset(confirm_ids))
118	
119	    def assert_confirm_only(self, ids: Iterable[str]) -> None:
120	        """Raise if ``ids`` is empty or any of it was seen during discovery (double-dip)."""
121	        ids = frozenset(ids)
122	        if not ids:
123	            raise FirewallViolation(
124	                "Firewall B: confirmatory test evaluated zero held-out objects — a verdict "
125	                "cannot be produced from an empty confirmation set (audit pass 1, issue #5)"
126	            )
127	        leaked = ids & self.discovery_ids
128	        if leaked:
129	            raise FirewallViolation(
130	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test touched {len(leaked)} discovery id(s) — "
131	                f"double-dipping, e.g. {sorted(leaked)[:3]}"
132	            )
133	        unknown = ids - self.confirm_ids
134	        if unknown:
135	            raise FirewallViolation(
136	                f"Firewall B: confirmatory test used {len(unknown)} id(s) outside the "
137	                f"confirmation partition, e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}"
138	            )
139

1	"""Firewalls are enforced in code, not prose (ADR-0005). These tests are the enforcement."""
2	
3	import pytest
4	
5	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
6	    ClaimBlindGuard,
7	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
8	    FirewallViolation,
9	)
10	
11	
12	def test_claim_blind_guard_blocks_access_while_sealed():
13	    guard = ClaimBlindGuard()
14	    claims = object()
15	    with guard.sealed():
16	        assert guard.is_sealed
17	        with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
18	            guard.access(claims)
19	    # released after the block — adjudication may now read claims
20	    assert guard.access(claims) is claims
21	
22	
23	def test_claim_blind_guard_restores_prior_state_on_nesting():
24	    guard = ClaimBlindGuard()
25	    with guard.sealed():
26	        with guard.sealed():
27	            assert guard.is_sealed
28	        assert guard.is_sealed  # inner exit must not unseal the outer block
29	    assert not guard.is_sealed
30	
31	
32	def test_split_rejects_overlapping_partitions():
33	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
34	        DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a", "b"}, {"b", "c"})
35	
36	
37	def test_confirm_only_rejects_discovery_ids():
38	    split = DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a", "b"}, {"c", "d"})
39	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
40	        split.assert_confirm_only({"a"})  # double-dipping on discovery data
41	
42	
43	def test_confirm_only_rejects_ids_outside_confirmation_partition():
44	    split = DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a"}, {"c", "d"})
45	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
46	        split.assert_confirm_only({"z"})
47	
48	
49	def test_confirm_only_accepts_held_out_ids():
50	    split = DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a", "b"}, {"c", "d"})
51	    split.assert_confirm_only({"c", "d"})  # no raise
52	
53	
54	def test_split_rejects_empty_confirmation_partition():
55	    # issue #5: a held-out split with nothing held out is invalid by construction
56	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
57	        DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a"}, set())
58	
59	
60	def test_confirm_only_rejects_empty_evaluated_ids():
61	    # issue #5: a confirmatory test that evaluated zero held-out objects must not pass
62	    split = DiscoverConfirmSplit.from_partition({"a"}, {"c", "d"})
63	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
64	        split.assert_confirm_only(set())
65	
66	
67	def test_custodial_guard_holds_and_releases_claims():
68	    # issue #1: the guard can take custody of the claims source; release() raises while sealed
69	    claims = object()
70	    guard = ClaimBlindGuard(claims)
71	    with guard.sealed():
72	        with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
73	            guard.release()
74	    assert guard.release() is claims
75	
76	
77	def test_custodial_guard_without_source_raises_on_release():
78	    guard = ClaimBlindGuard()
79	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
80	        guard.release()
81

content.text: The firewall tests look reasonably discriminating. Let me look at the methodology core (confirm, adjudication, discovery) and the determinism/concurrency areas.

content.content:
1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations)."""
46	    units = [
47	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
48	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
49	    ]
50	    relations = [
51	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
52	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
53	    ]
54	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
55	    return hash_text(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False))
56	
57	
58	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
59	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
60	
61	    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
62	    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
63	    """
64	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
65	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
69	class DeterminismRecord:
70	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
71	
72	    seed: int
73	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
74	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
77	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
78	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
79	
80	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
81	        return {
82	            "seed": self.seed,
83	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
84	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
85	        }
86	
87	
88	def build_baseline_artifact(
89	    corpus: Corpus,
90	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
91	    *,
92	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
93	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
94	    seed: int | None = None,
95	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
96	) -> BaselineArtifact:
97	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
98	
99	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
100	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
101	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
102	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
103	    so it is reproducible. ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed
104	    :func:`corpus_fingerprint` to avoid recomputing it (#119).
105	    """
106	    # Reuse a precomputed fingerprint if the caller already has one (e.g. the runner computed it
107	    # at ingest), avoiding a redundant full canonical-hash of the whole corpus (#119).
108	    corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
109	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
110	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
111	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
112	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
113	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
114	
115	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
116	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
117	
118	    if seed is None:
119	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
120	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
121	
122	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
123	    return BaselineArtifact(
124	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
125	    )
126

1	"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
4	mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:
5	
6	- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
7	  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
8	- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
9	  require stability across resamples.
10	
11	This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
12	are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
13	refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
14	statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
15	``indeterminate``).
16	
17	Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
18	particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
19	claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
20	opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
21	upper-tail-only implementation.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import datetime as _dt
27	import math
28	from statistics import median
29	from typing import Iterable, Sequence
30	
31	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
32	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
33	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
34	    TimestampAuthority,
35	    require_preregistered,
36	)
37	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
38	
39	
40	def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
41	    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.
42	
43	    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
44	    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
45	    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
46	    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
47	    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
48	    """
49	    if authority is not None:
50	        require_preregistered(
51	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
52	        )
53	    else:
54	        require_locked(hypothesis)
55	
56	
57	def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
58	    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.
59	
60	    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
61	    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
62	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
63	    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
64	    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
65	    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
66	    """
67	    if not hypothesis.locked:
68	        raise FirewallViolation(
69	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
70	            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
71	        )
72	
73	
74	def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
75	    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
76	    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
77	    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
78	    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
79	        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")
80	
81	
82	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
83	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
84	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
85	
86	
87	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
88	    if not math.isfinite(value):
89	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
90	
91	
92	def verdict_from_pvalue(
93	    hypothesis_id: str,
94	    *,
95	    statistic: float,
96	    p_value: float,
97	    alpha: float,
98	    powered: bool,
99	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
100	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
101	) -> Verdict:
102	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
103	
104	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
105	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
106	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
107	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
108	
109	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
110	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
111	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
112	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
113	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
114	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
115	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
116	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
117	    """
118	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
119	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
120	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
121	
122	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
123	    common = dict(
124	        statistic=statistic,
125	        threshold=alpha,
126	        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
127	    )
128	    if not powered:
129	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
130	    if p_value <= alpha:
131	        if direction_supports_claim:
132	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
133	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
134	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
135	
136	
137	def confirm_unit_level(
138	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
139	    *,
140	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
141	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
142	    statistic: float,
143	    p_value: float,
144	    alpha: float,
145	    powered: bool,
146	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
147	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
148	) -> Verdict:
149	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
150	
151	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
152	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
153	    """
154	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
155	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
156	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
157	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
158	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
159	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
160	        statistic=statistic,
161	        p_value=p_value,
162	        alpha=alpha,
163	        powered=powered,
164	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
165	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
166	    )
167	
168	
169	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
170	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
171	    n = len(null)
172	    if tail == "greater":
173	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
174	    else:
175	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
176	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
177	
178	
179	def _resample_stability(resamples: Sequence[float], reference: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
180	    """Fraction of resampled statistics that fall on the predicted side of ``reference``.
181	
182	    A measured stand-in for "the effect direction reproduces across resamples": the closer
183	    to 1.0, the more consistently the resampled statistic stays in the predicted tail.
184	    """
185	    if tail == "greater":
186	        agree = sum(1 for r in resamples if r > reference)
187	    else:
188	        agree = sum(1 for r in resamples if r < reference)
189	    return agree / len(resamples)
190	
191	
192	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
193	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time
194	    argument is optional and, if given, must match the pre-registered one."""
195	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
196	    if locked_dir is not None:
197	        if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
198	            raise ValueError(
199	                "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
200	                f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
201	            )
202	        return locked_dir
203	    if predicted_direction is None:
204	        raise ValueError(
205	            "no predicted_direction: set it on the hypothesis at pre-registration "
206	            "(preferred) or pass it explicitly"
207	        )
208	    return predicted_direction
209	
210	
211	def confirm_whole_corpus(
212	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
213	    *,
214	    observed: float,
215	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
216	    alpha: float,
217	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
218	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
219	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
220	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
221	) -> Verdict:
222	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
223	
224	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
225	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
226	
227	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
228	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
229	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
230	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
231	
232	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
233	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
234	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
235	
236	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
237	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
238	    """
239	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
240	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
241	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
242	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
243	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
244	    if not null_distribution:
245	        raise ValueError(
246	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
247	        )
248	    for v in null_distribution:
249	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
250	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
251	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
252	
253	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
254	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
255	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
256	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
257	    n = len(null_distribution)
258	
259	    stability: float | None = None
260	    if resample_statistics is not None:
261	        if not resample_statistics:
262	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
263	        for r in resample_statistics:
264	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
265	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, median(null_distribution), direction)
266	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
267	
268	    rule = (
269	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
270	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
271	    )
272	    evidence = {
273	        "p_support": p_support,
274	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
275	        "null_n": n,
276	        "predicted_direction": direction,
277	        "stability": stability,
278	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
279	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
280	    }
281	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
282	
283	    # A contradiction (significant in the opposite tail) is reportable without stability.
284	    if p_contra <= alpha:
285	        return Verdict.contradicted(
286	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
287	        )
288	    if stability is None:
289	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
290	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
291	            rule,
292	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
293	            **common,
294	        )
295	    if not stable:
296	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
297	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
298	        )
299	    if p_support <= alpha:
300	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
301	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
302	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
303	    )
304

content.content:
1	"""Confirmatory machinery: locking gate, three-verdict mapping, direction-aware null test,
2	input validation, and the unit-level path (audit pass 1: #2, #4, #5, #17)."""
3	
4	import pytest
5	
6	from assay_engine.methodology.confirm import (
7	    confirm_unit_level,
8	    confirm_whole_corpus,
9	    require_locked,
10	    verdict_from_pvalue,
11	)
12	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
13	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import (
14	    Hypothesis,
15	    HypothesisKind,
16	    HypothesisOrigin,
17	)
18	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import VerdictLabel
19	
20	
21	def _locked(kind: HypothesisKind, hid: str = "h1", predicted_direction=None) -> Hypothesis:
22	    return Hypothesis(
23	        hypothesis_id=hid,
24	        statement="x",
25	        kind=kind,
26	        origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
27	        test_name="t",
28	        decision_rule="r",
29	        locked_at="2026-06-15T00:00:00Z",
30	        timestamp_proof="rfc3161:deadbeef",
31	        predicted_direction=predicted_direction,
32	    )
33	
34	
35	def _unlocked(kind: HypothesisKind) -> Hypothesis:
36	    return Hypothesis(
37	        hypothesis_id="h0",
38	        statement="x",
39	        kind=kind,
40	        origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
41	        test_name="t",
42	        decision_rule="r",
43	    )
44	
45	
46	# ---- locking gate ----
47	
48	
49	def test_require_locked_blocks_unlocked_hypothesis():
50	    assert not _unlocked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS).locked
51	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
52	        require_locked(_unlocked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS))
53	
54	
55	# ---- verdict_from_pvalue: three outcomes + validation ----
56	
57	
58	def test_verdict_mapping_covers_three_outcomes():
59	    assert (
60	        verdict_from_pvalue(
61	            "h",
62	            statistic=3.0,
63	            p_value=0.01,
64	            alpha=0.05,
65	            powered=True,
66	            direction_supports_claim=True,
67	        ).label
68	        is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
69	    )
70	    assert (
71	        verdict_from_pvalue(
72	            "h",
73	            statistic=3.0,
74	            p_value=0.01,
75	            alpha=0.05,
76	            powered=True,
77	            direction_supports_claim=False,
78	        ).label
79	        is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
80	    )
81	    assert (
82	        verdict_from_pvalue(
83	            "h",
84	            statistic=0.1,
85	            p_value=0.4,
86	            alpha=0.05,
87	            powered=False,
88	            direction_supports_claim=True,
89	        ).label
90	        is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
91	    )
92	    assert (
93	        verdict_from_pvalue(
94	            "h",
95	            statistic=0.1,
96	            p_value=0.4,
97	            alpha=0.05,
98	            powered=True,
99	            direction_supports_claim=True,
100	        ).label
101	        is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
102	    )
103	
104	
105	@pytest.mark.parametrize(
106	    "kw",
107	    [
108	        {"p_value": -0.1},
109	        {"p_value": 1.5},
110	        {"alpha": 0.0},
111	        {"alpha": 0.5},
112	        {"alpha": 1.0},
113	        {"statistic": float("nan")},
114	        {"statistic": float("inf")},
115	    ],
116	)
117	def test_verdict_from_pvalue_validates_inputs(kw):
118	    base = dict(
119	        statistic=1.0, p_value=0.01, alpha=0.05, powered=True, direction_supports_claim=True
120	    )
121	    base.update(kw)
122	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
123	        verdict_from_pvalue("h", **base)
124	
125	
126	def test_rule_string_is_not_hardcoded_two_sided():
127	    v = verdict_from_pvalue(
128	        "h",
129	        statistic=1.0,
130	        p_value=0.01,
131	        alpha=0.05,
132	        powered=True,
133	        direction_supports_claim=True,
134	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
135	    )
136	    assert "two-sided" not in v.decision_rule
137	    assert "held-out unit-level test" in v.decision_rule
138	
139	
140	# ---- confirm_whole_corpus: direction-aware (#2) + engine-measured stability (#21) ----
141	
142	_STABLE_HI = [100.0] * 20  # resamples far above any small null -> stable for "greater"
143	_STABLE_LO = [-100.0] * 20  # far below -> stable for "less"
144	
145	
146	def test_whole_corpus_supported_upper_tail():
147	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
148	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
149	        observed=10.0,
150	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
151	        alpha=0.05,
152	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
153	        predicted_direction="greater",
154	    )
155	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
156	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 1.0  # engine-measured, not asserted (#21)
157	
158	
159	def test_whole_corpus_supported_lower_tail():
160	    # A claim predicting a LOW statistic, observed far below the null, must be SUPPORTED
161	    # (previously returned INDETERMINATE under direction-blind upper-tail-only logic).
162	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
163	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
164	        observed=0.0,
165	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
166	        alpha=0.05,
167	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
168	        predicted_direction="less",
169	    )
170	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
171	
172	
173	def test_whole_corpus_contradicted_when_significant_in_opposite_tail():
174	    # Claim predicts HIGH, but observed is far below the null -> a real contradiction
175	    # (reportable without resamples; a contradiction does not need stability).
176	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
177	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
178	        observed=0.0,
179	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
180	        alpha=0.05,
181	        predicted_direction="greater",
182	    )
183	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
184	
185	
186	def test_whole_corpus_stability_not_assessed_without_resamples():
187	    # issue #21: SUPPORTED is unavailable if stability was never measured.
188	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
189	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
190	        observed=10.0,
191	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
192	        alpha=0.05,
193	        predicted_direction="greater",
194	    )
195	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
196	    assert v.evidence["stability"] is None
197	
198	
199	def test_whole_corpus_unstable_resamples_is_indeterminate_even_if_beats_null():
200	    # issue #21: resamples that don't reproduce the direction -> not stable -> indeterminate.
201	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
202	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
203	        observed=10.0,
204	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
205	        alpha=0.05,
206	        resample_statistics=[-1.0] * 20,
207	        predicted_direction="greater",
208	    )
209	    assert v.is_indeterminate
210	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 0.0
211	
212	
213	def test_whole_corpus_not_significant_is_indeterminate():
214	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
215	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
216	        observed=5.0,
217	        null_distribution=list(range(100)),
218	        alpha=0.01,
219	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
220	        predicted_direction="greater",
221	    )
222	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE
223	
224	
225	def test_whole_corpus_uses_pre_registered_direction():
226	    # issue #24: direction fixed on the locked hypothesis; no confirm-time arg needed.
227	    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="less")
228	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
229	        h,
230	        observed=0.0,
231	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
232	        alpha=0.05,
233	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
234	    )
235	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
236	
237	
238	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_direction_conflict_with_lock():
239	    # issue #24: a confirm-time direction contradicting the pre-registered one is refused.
240	    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="less")
241	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
242	        confirm_whole_corpus(
243	            h,
244	            observed=0.0,
245	            null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
246	            alpha=0.05,
247	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,
248	            predicted_direction="greater",
249	        )
250	
251

content.text: I notice the confirm_whole_corpus has a subtle issue worth checking: when `p_contra <= alpha` it returns contradicted FIRST, even before checking stability. But there's a deeper concern. Let me check the persistence (concurrency) and adjudication tests, which are the highest-risk areas. Let me also examine the determinism test.

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping. The PG advisory lock serializes DDL
56	# across PROCESSES; this lock makes the in-process check-and-set sequences (atexit
57	# registration, the initialized-conn set) atomic so two threads can't double-register or
58	# double-setup.
59	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
60	
61	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
62	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
63	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
64	_atexit_registered = False
65	
66	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
67	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
68	# first-time cross-process race.
69	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
70	
71	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
72	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
73	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
74	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
75	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
76	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
77	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
78	
79	
80	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
81	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
82	
83	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
84	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
85	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
89	        host = u.hostname or ""
90	        port = u.port
91	        scheme = u.scheme
92	        path = u.path
93	    except ValueError:
94	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
95	    if not host:
96	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
97	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
98	        host = f"[{host}]"
99	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
100	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
101	
102	
103	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
104	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
105	
106	    Layers, in order of precision:
107	
108	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
109	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
110	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
111	       *other* URI in the text;
112	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
113	
114	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
115	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
116	    """
117	    if conn_str:
118	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
119	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
120	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
121	
122	
123	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
124	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
125	
126	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
127	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
128	    """
129	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
130	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
131	
132	
133	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
134	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
135	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
136	    that can raise)."""
137	    try:
138	        pool.close()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
144	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
145	        _safe_close(p)
146	
147	
148	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    global _atexit_registered
150	    with _init_lock:
151	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
152	        if not _atexit_registered:
153	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
154	            _atexit_registered = True
155	
156	
157	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
158	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
159	
160	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
161	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
162	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
163	    or the extra is not installed.
164	    """
165	    try:
166	        if use_memory:
167	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
168	
169	            return MemorySaver()
170	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
171	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
172	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
173	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
174	        raise RuntimeError(
175	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
176	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
177	        ) from exc
178	
179	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
180	    failure: str | None = None
181	    pool: Any = None
182	    try:
183	        pool = ConnectionPool(
184	            conn_str,
185	            min_size=1,
186	            max_size=8,
187	            kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
188	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
189	            open=True,
190	        )
191	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
192	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
193	        # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
194	        # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
195	        with _init_lock:
196	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
197	                with pool.connection() as conn:
198	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	                    try:
200	                        # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
201	                        # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
202	                        # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
203	                        PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
204	                    finally:
205	                        # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
206	                        # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
207	                        try:
208	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
209	                        except Exception:
210	                            pass
211	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
212	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
213	    except Exception as exc:
214	        # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
215	        # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
216	        # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
217	        failure = (
218	            f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
219	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
220	            "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
221	        )
222	        # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
223	        # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
224	        if pool is not None:
225	            _safe_close(pool)
226	            with _init_lock:
227	                if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
228	                    _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
229	    raise RuntimeError(failure)
230	
231	
232	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
233	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
234	
235	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
236	    otherwise Postgres.
237	    """
238	    if use_memory is None:
239	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
240	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
241

content.text: Now let me look at the persistence tests (concurrency guards) and adjudication. These are high-priority security/concurrency areas.

274	
275	    class FakeClient:
276	        def __init__(self):
277	            self.batches = []
278	            self.closed = False
279	
280	        def upsert(self, collection, points):
281	            self.batches.append(len(points))
282	
283	        def close(self):
284	            self.closed = True
285	
286	    fc = FakeClient()
287	    store = QdrantVectorStore("c", 2, client=fc)
288	    store.upsert([f"u{i}" for i in range(5)], [[0.0, 1.0]] * 5, batch_size=2)
289	    assert fc.batches == [2, 2, 1]  # #118: streamed in bounded batches
290	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
291	        store.upsert(["u0"], [[0.0], [1.0]])  # length mismatch
292	    store.close()
293	    assert fc.closed is False  # #117: an INJECTED client is the caller's to close, not ours

296	def test_vector_store_closes_owned_client(monkeypatch):
297	    # #117: a self-created client (no client injected) MUST be closed on close()/context-exit
298	    pytest.importorskip("qdrant_client")
299	    from assay_engine.persistence import vectorstore as vs
300	
301	    class FakeClient:
302	        def __init__(self):
303	            self.closed = False
304	
305	        def close(self):
306	            self.closed = True
307	
308	    created = FakeClient()
309	    monkeypatch.setattr(vs, "get_qdrant_client", lambda: created)
310	    with vs.QdrantVectorStore("c", 2) as store:  # no client injected → owns it
311	        assert store._owns_client is True
312	    assert created.closed is True  # owned client closed on context exit (leak prevented)

315	def test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time():
316	    # #118: only one batch of PointStructs may be materialized at a time — assert construction is
317	    # interleaved with sends, not all-up-front.
318	    pytest.importorskip("qdrant_client")
319	    from assay_engine.persistence.vectorstore import QdrantVectorStore
320	
321	    accessed: list[int] = []
322	
323	    class TrackingVectors:
324	        def __init__(self, data):
325	            self._d = data
326	
327	        def __len__(self):
328	            return len(self._d)
329	
330	        def __getitem__(self, i):
331	            accessed.append(i)  # records when each vector is consumed
332	            return self._d[i]
333	
334	    class FakeClient:
335	        def __init__(self):
336	            self.accessed_at_call = []
337	
338	        def upsert(self, collection, points):
339	            self.accessed_at_call.append(len(accessed))  # how many vectors consumed so far
340	
341	    fc = FakeClient()
342	    store = QdrantVectorStore("c", 2, client=fc)
343	    store.upsert([f"u{i}" for i in range(6)], TrackingVectors([[0.0, 1.0]] * 6), batch_size=2)
344	    # per-batch construction → 2,4,6 consumed at each send; all-up-front would be 6,6,6
345	    assert fc.accessed_at_call == [2, 4, 6]
346	
347	
348	# ---- hardened checkpointer invariants (fake backends injected, no Postgres) ----
349	
350	
351	@pytest.fixture
352	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
353	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
354	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
355	    import sys
356	    import types
357	
358	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
359	
360	    state = {"setup_on": [], "lock_on": [], "pools": [], "atexit_registered": 0}
361	
362	    class FakeConn:
363	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
364	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
365	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
366	
367	    class FakePool:
368	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
369	
370	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
371	            self.conn_str = conn_str
372	            self._conn = FakeConn()
373	            state["pools"].append(self)
374	
375	        def connection(self):
376	            conn = self._conn
377	
378	            class _Ctx:
379	                def __enter__(self_):
380	                    return conn
381	
382	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
383	                    return False
384	
385	            return _Ctx()
386	
387	        def close(self):
388	            pass
389	
390	    class FakeSaver:
391	        def __init__(self, arg):
392	            self.arg = arg
393	
394	        def setup(self):
395	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
396	
397	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
398	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
399	        for k, v in attrs.items():
400	            setattr(m, k, v)
401	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
402	        return m
403	
404	    _mod("langgraph")
405	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
406	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
407	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
408	    _mod("psycopg")
409	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
410	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
411	
412	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
413	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS", set())
414	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
415	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
416	    monkeypatch.setattr(
417	        cp,
418	        "atexit",
419	        types.SimpleNamespace(
420	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
421	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
422	            )
423	        ),
424	    )
425	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
426	    return cp, state
427	
428	
429	def test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection(fake_pg):
430	    cp, state = fake_pg
431	    cp.get_checkpointer()
432	    cp.get_checkpointer()  # second call, same conn_str
433	    # setup() ran exactly once despite two factory calls (once-per-conn guard)
434	    assert len(state["setup_on"]) == 1
435	    # the advisory lock and the DDL ran on the SAME connection object
436	    assert state["lock_on"] == state["setup_on"]
437	
438	
439	def test_atexit_pool_cleanup_registered_once(fake_pg):
440	    cp, state = fake_pg
441	    cp.get_checkpointer()
442	    cp.get_checkpointer()
443	    # two pools opened and tracked, but only one shared atexit handler registered
444	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 2
445	    assert len(cp._OPEN_POOLS) == 2
446	    assert state["atexit_registered"] == 1
447	
448	
449	def test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails(fake_pg):
450	    # #105: if schema bootstrap fails AFTER the pool opened, the pool must be closed and dropped
451	    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.
452	    import sys
453	
454	    cp, state = fake_pg
455	    closed: list = []
456	    Pool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
457	    orig_close = Pool.close
458	    Pool.close = lambda self: (closed.append(self), orig_close(self))[1]  # record close
459	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
460	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
461	
462	    def boom(self):
463	        raise RuntimeError("setup failed (schema DDL)")
464	
465	    Saver.setup = boom
466	    try:
467	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="connection failed"):
468	            cp.get_checkpointer()
469	        assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # a pool was opened
470	        assert closed and closed[0] is state["pools"][0]  # and it was closed
471	        assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # and removed from the cleanup registry (no leak)
472	    finally:
473	        Pool.close = orig_close
474	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
475	
476	
477	def test_connection_failure_redacts_and_leaks_no_context(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
478	    # issue #P2: on failure the credential-bearing original must be retained as neither
479	    # __cause__ nor __context__, and the message must be redacted.
480	    import sys
481	
482	    cp, _ = fake_pg
483	    conn = "postgresql://admin:SUPERSECRET@127.0.0.1:5432/x"
484	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", conn)
485	
486	    def boom(*a, **k):
487	        raise OSError(f"could not connect using {conn}")
488	
489	    monkeypatch.setattr(sys.modules["psycopg_pool"], "ConnectionPool", boom)
490	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError) as ei:
491	        cp.get_checkpointer()
492	    err = ei.value
493	    assert "SUPERSECRET" not in str(err)
494	    assert err.__cause__ is None and err.__context__ is None  # no credential-bearing original
495	
496	
497	def test_versioner_publish_leaves_no_temp_files(tmp_path):
498	    # atomic publish must not leave .tmp- scratch behind, and an empty file is versionable
499	    store = tmp_path / "store"
500	    empty = tmp_path / "empty.bin"
501	    empty.write_bytes(b"")
502	    v = LocalDataVersioner(store_dir=str(store))
503	    digest = v.put(str(empty))
504	    assert v.path_for(digest).is_file()
505	    leftovers = [p.name for p in store.rglob(".tmp-*")]
506	    assert leftovers == []
507

content.text:
The persistence tests look quite thorough. Now I notice the concurrency claim about `setup()` being atomic under concurrent threads — the test `test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection` only calls sequentially, never concurrently. Let me check the `_init_lock` claim. But the lock is held during setup, so sequential test is fine for the once-per-conn guard.

Let me look at adjudication, discovery, preregistration, and provenance which are core methodology guards. Let me also check the reasoning seam.

1	"""Adjudication runner — Firewall A enforced by construction + the source scorecard (#ADR-0008).
2	
3	These tests are the firewall's enforcement, not a description of it: a baseline builder that
4	peeks at the claims during construction must be STRUCTURALLY stopped, and the source scorecard
5	must honestly reflect the verdicts.
6	"""
7	
8	import datetime as _dt
9	
10	import pytest
11	
12	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
13	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
14	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus, Unit
15	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import adjudicate
16	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard, FirewallViolation
17	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
18	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import LocalHmacAuthority, lock_hypothesis
19	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict, VerdictLabel
20	
21	# A real (data-sovereign) pre-registration authority for the tests; the runner now verifies
22	# the lock, so hypotheses must be genuinely locked, not hand-stamped with a fake proof.
23	_AUTH = LocalHmacAuthority(b"adjudication-test-secret-key-0001")
24	# Lock comfortably in the past so it is always strictly before the runner's confirmation moment.
25	_LOCK_INSTANT = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc) - _dt.timedelta(hours=1)
26	
27	
28	class _Claims:
29	    """A minimal ExternalClaimsSource over a fixed list of claims."""
30	
31	    def __init__(self, claims):
32	        self._claims = claims
33	
34	    def claims(self):
35	        return list(self._claims)
36	
37	    def claim_fingerprint(self):
38	        return "claims-fp"
39	
40	
41	def _claim(cid, supported=True):
42	    # 'assertion.expected' carries what the source asserts; the confirmer compares to baseline.
43	    return ClaimRecord(
44	        claim_id=cid,
45	        subject=cid,
46	        referents=(cid,),
47	        assertion={"expected": "high" if supported else "low"},
48	    )
49	
50	
51	def _corpus():
52	    return Corpus(units=(Unit("a", "x"), Unit("b", "y")))
53	
54	
55	def _hypothesis_for(claim: ClaimRecord) -> Hypothesis:
56	    return lock_hypothesis(
57	        Hypothesis(
58	            hypothesis_id=f"H-{claim.claim_id}",
59	            statement="claim holds against the baseline",
60	            kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,
61	            origin=HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM,
62	            test_name="adjudication",
63	            decision_rule="baseline corroborates the asserted relationship",
64	            source_claim_id=claim.claim_id,
65	        ),
66	        authority=_AUTH,
67	        instant=_LOCK_INSTANT,
68	    )
69	
70	
71	class _GoodBuilder:
72	    """A well-behaved baseline builder: uses only the corpus, never the claims."""
73	
74	    def build(self, corpus, *, claim_guard):
75	        return BaselineArtifact(corpus_fingerprint="fp", contents={"n_units": len(corpus.units)})
76	
77	
78	class _SneakyBuilder:
79	    """A builder that tries to peek at the claims during construction — must be stopped."""
80	
81	    def build(self, corpus, *, claim_guard):
82	        claim_guard.release()  # Firewall A violation: claims are sealed during the build
83	        return BaselineArtifact(corpus_fingerprint="fp", contents={})
84	
85	
86	def _confirmer(label_for=lambda cid: VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED):
87	    def confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim):
88	        assert isinstance(baseline, BaselineArtifact)  # the real artifact is passed through
89	        # the verdict must report the hypothesis it answers (runner checks this identity)
90	        return Verdict(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, label_for(claim.claim_id), "r")
91	
92	    return confirm
93	
94	
95	# ---- Firewall A is enforced by construction ----
96	
97	
98	def test_sneaky_builder_cannot_read_claims_during_build():
99	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
100	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation):
101	        adjudicate(
102	            _corpus(),
103	            claims,
104	            baseline_builder=_SneakyBuilder(),
105	            hypothesis_for=_hypothesis_for,
106	            authority=_AUTH,
107	            confirm=_confirmer(),
108	        )
109	
110	
111	def test_builder_is_handed_no_claims_source():
112	    # signature-level Firewall A (the honest guarantee, ADR-0008): the builder is never handed
113	    # a claims source and the guard it receives holds nothing — so it cannot ACCIDENTALLY
114	    # consult the claims. (Deliberate sys._getframe reflection into the runner's stack is out
115	    # of scope; only process isolation could stop that, per ADR-0008.)
116	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
117	    seen = {}
118	
119	    class _InspectingBuilder:
120	        def build(self, corpus, *, claim_guard):
121	            seen["guard_holds"] = getattr(claim_guard, "_claims_source", None)
122	            return BaselineArtifact(corpus_fingerprint="fp", contents={})
123	
124	    adjudicate(
125	        _corpus(),
126	        claims,
127	        baseline_builder=_InspectingBuilder(),
128	        hypothesis_for=_hypothesis_for,
129	        authority=_AUTH,
130	        confirm=_confirmer(),
131	    )
132	    assert seen["guard_holds"] is None  # the guard handed to the builder holds no claims
133	
134	
135	def test_good_builder_never_receives_claims_but_adjudication_then_sees_them():
136	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1"), _claim("c2")])
137	    captured = {"baseline_contents": None, "confirmed": []}
138	
139	    builder = _GoodBuilder()
140	
141	    def confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim):
142	        captured["baseline_contents"] = dict(baseline.contents)
143	        captured["confirmed"].append(claim.claim_id)
144	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, "r")
145	
146	    baseline, scorecard = adjudicate(
147	        _corpus(),

150	        hypothesis_for=_hypothesis_for,
151	        authority=_AUTH,
152	        confirm=confirm,
153	        source_name="src",
154	    )
155	    assert captured["baseline_contents"] == {"n_units": 2}  # baseline built from corpus only
156	    assert captured["confirmed"] == ["c1", "c2"]  # claims adjudicated AFTER the build
157	    assert scorecard.total == 2
158	
159	
160	def test_non_external_claim_hypothesis_is_rejected():
161	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
162	
163	    def discovery_hypothesis(claim):  # wrong: a data-surfaced hypothesis, not a pre-stated claim
164	        h = _hypothesis_for(claim)
165	        return Hypothesis(

168	            kind=h.kind,
169	            origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
170	            test_name=h.test_name,
171	            decision_rule=h.decision_rule,
172	            locked_at=h.locked_at,
173	            timestamp_proof=h.timestamp_proof,
174	        )
175	
176	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="EXTERNAL_CLAIM"):

183	            confirm=_confirmer(),
184	        )
185	
186	
187	# ---- claim<->hypothesis<->verdict identity (BREAK 2: no silent misattribution) ----
188	
189	
190	def test_runner_rejects_mismatched_source_claim_id():
191	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
192	
193	    def wrong_source(claim):  # bug: hard-codes a different source_claim_id
194	        h = _hypothesis_for(claim)
195	        return Hypothesis(
196	            hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
197	            statement=h.statement,
198	            kind=h.kind,
199	            origin=h.origin,
200	            test_name=h.test_name,
201	            decision_rule=h.decision_rule,
202	            source_claim_id="WRONG",
203	            locked_at=h.locked_at,
204	            timestamp_proof=h.timestamp_proof,
205	        )
206	
207	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="misattribution"):
208	        adjudicate(
209	            _corpus(),
210	            claims,
211	            baseline_builder=_GoodBuilder(),
212	            hypothesis_for=wrong_source,
213	            authority=_AUTH,
214	            confirm=_confirmer(),
215	        )
216	
217	
218	def test_runner_rejects_verdict_for_wrong_hypothesis():
219	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
220	
221	    def mislabeled(hypothesis, baseline, claim):  # verdict reports the wrong hypothesis_id
222	        return Verdict.supported("SOME-OTHER-H", "r")
223	
224	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="misattribution"):
225	        adjudicate(
226	            _corpus(),
227	            claims,
228	            baseline_builder=_GoodBuilder(),
229	            hypothesis_for=_hypothesis_for,
230	            authority=_AUTH,
231	            confirm=mislabeled,
232	        )
233	
234	
235	def test_runner_rejects_hypothesis_locked_after_baseline():
236	    # #83: adjudicate captures not_after BEFORE the baseline build, so a hypothesis locked
237	    # on demand (i.e. after the baseline/answer exists) is rejected — claim-derived hypotheses
238	    # must be pre-locked before the baseline.
239	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
240	
241	    def lock_on_demand(claim):  # locks at "now", which is after the runner captured not_after
242	        h = Hypothesis(
243	            hypothesis_id=f"H-{claim.claim_id}",
244	            statement="s",
245	            kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,
246	            origin=HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM,
247	            test_name="t",
248	            decision_rule="r",
249	            source_claim_id=claim.claim_id,
250	        )
251	        return lock_hypothesis(h, authority=_AUTH)  # default instant = now()
252	
253	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="precede|strictly before"):
254	        adjudicate(
255	            _corpus(),
256	            claims,
257	            baseline_builder=_GoodBuilder(),
258	            hypothesis_for=lock_on_demand,
259	            authority=_AUTH,
260	            confirm=_confirmer(),
261	        )
262	
263	
264	def test_runner_rejects_unlocked_hypothesis():
265	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])

267	    def unlocked(claim):  # not pre-registered: no lock/timestamp
268	        h = _hypothesis_for(claim)
269	        return Hypothesis(

285	            authority=_AUTH,
286	            confirm=_confirmer(),

290	# ---- the source scorecard (METHODOLOGY.md §5) ----
291	
292	
293	def test_scorecard_counts_and_alignment_rate_excludes_indeterminate():
294	    verdict_by = {
295	        "c1": VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED,

316	    assert sc.alignment_rate == pytest.approx(2 / 3)  # indeterminate excluded from denominator
317	    assert sc.source == "SCF-like"

321	    claims = _Claims([_claim("c1")])
